claims.text: Label projection is an effective technique for cross-lingual transfer.
claims.source_quote: Label projection is an effective technique for cross-lingual transfer, extending span-annotated datasets from a high-resource language to low-resource ones.

claims.text: Label projection extends span-annotated datasets from a high-resource language to low-resource ones.
claims.source_quote: Label projection is an effective technique for cross-lingual transfer, extending span-annotated datasets from a high-resource language to low-resource ones.

claims.text: Most label projection approaches perform label projection as a separate step after machine translation.
claims.source_quote: Most approaches perform label projection as a separate step after machine translation, and prior work that combines the two reports degraded translation quality.

claims.text: Prior work that combines translation and label projection reports degraded translation quality.
claims.source_quote: prior work that combines the two reports degraded translation quality.

claims.text: LabelPigeon jointly performs translation and label projection via XML tags.
claims.source_quote: We re-evaluate this claim with LabelPigeon, a novel framework that jointly performs translation and label projection via XML tags.

claims.text: LabelPigeon outperforms baselines in label projection across 11 languages.
claims.source_quote: find that LabelPigeon outperforms baselines and actively improves translation quality in 11 languages.

claims.text: LabelPigeon actively improves translation quality in 11 languages.
claims.source_quote: find that LabelPigeon outperforms baselines and actively improves translation quality in 11 languages.

claims.text: Translation quality was assessed across 203 languages using LabelPigeon.
claims.source_quote: We further assess translation quality across 203 languages and varying annotation complexity, finding consistent improvement attributed to additional fine-tuning.

claims.text: LabelPigeon shows consistent improvement in translation quality across 203 languages.
claims.source_quote: finding consistent improvement attributed to additional fine-tuning.

claims.text: The consistent improvement in translation quality is attributed to additional fine-tuning.
claims.source_quote: finding consistent improvement attributed to additional fine-tuning.

claims.text: LabelPigeon was evaluated across 27 languages and three downstream tasks.
claims.source_quote: across 27 languages and three downstream tasks, we report substantial gains in cross-lingual transfer over comparable work

claims.text: LabelPigeon achieves up to +39.9 F1 improvement on NER over comparable work.
claims.source_quote: we report substantial gains in cross-lingual transfer over comparable work, up to +39.9 F1 on NER.

claims.text: XML-tagged label projection does not compromise translation quality.
claims.source_quote: XML-tagged label projection provides effective and efficient label transfer without compromising translation quality.

claims.text: XML-tagged label projection provides effective label transfer.
claims.source_quote: XML-tagged label projection provides effective and efficient label transfer without compromising translation quality.

claims.text: XML-tagged label projection provides efficient label transfer.
claims.source_quote: XML-tagged label projection provides effective and efficient label transfer without compromising translation quality.

claims.text: LabelPigeon uses a direct evaluation scheme for label projection.
claims.source_quote: We design a direct evaluation scheme for label projection
